titleStmt.title: SII 1.59: original edition by Eugen Julius Theodor Hultzsch
No. 59. ON THE BASE OF THE NORTH WALL

summary.p: This inscription is dated in the twenty-second year of Tribhuvanachakravartin RńĀjarńĀja-deva and in the Ňöaka year 1160. It records the gift of the village of KumńĀramaŠĻÖgalam, which was situated east of KoŠĻüŠĻüa-maŠĻÖgalam, north-west of AimbŇęŠĻáŠłći‚ÄĒ which lay to the north of Poygai, alias RńĀjendra-ChoŠł∑a-nallŇęr‚ÄĒand south of the PńĀlaŠĻüu. AimbŇęŠĻáŠłći is the old name of the modern village of AmmuŠĻáŠłći; it occurs also in an AmmuŠĻáŠłći inscription, which will be noticed in Part III (No. 131). The PńĀlńĀŠĻüu is the well-known PńĀlńĀr, the chief river of the North Arcot District.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti Ňõrńę . cakaraiyńĀŠĻáŠĻ≠u ńÄyirattoruŠĻČŇęŠĻüŠĻüaŠĻüupatu cellńĀŠĻČiŠĻČŠĻüa ŇõrńętiripuvaŠĻČaccakkaravattikaŠł∑ ŇõrńęrńĀjarńĀjadevaŠĻüku yńĀ
ŠĻáŠĻ≠u IrupattiraŠĻáŠĻ≠ńĀvatu tai poykai ńÄŠĻČa IrńĀcentiracoŠłĽanallŇęrccittirameŠłĽimalaimaŠĻáŠĻ≠alaviŠĻáŠĻáakarńĀ
ŠĻČa AruŠł∑ńĀŠł∑apperumńĀŠł∑ukkuttiruviŠĻ≠aiyńĀŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠amńĀkakkumńĀramaŠĻÖkalam . melpńĀŠĻükellai koŠĻüŠĻüamaŠĻÖkalattu Ellai ńÄcaŠĻüutiyiŠĻČaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠a tiruvńĀŠłĽikkallukku kiŠłĽak
kum . teŠĻČpńĀŠĻükellai poykaiyńĀŠĻČa IrńĀcentiracoŠłĽanallŇęrkku vaŠĻ≠akkńĀŠĻČa AimpŇęŠĻáŠĻ≠i kayakkńĀlukku vaŠĻ≠akkum . kiŠłĽpńĀŠĻükellai AimpŇęŠĻáŠĻ≠i Ellai ńÄcaŠĻüutiyil naŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠a ti
ruvńĀŠłĽikkallukku meŠĻükkum . vaŠĻ≠apńĀŠĻükkellai pńĀlńĀŠĻüŠĻüukku teŠĻükku minta ŠĻČńĀŠĻüppńĀŠĻükkellaikkumuŠĻ≠paŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠a meŠĻČokkiŠĻČa maramum kiŠĻáokkiŠĻČa kiŠĻáaŠĻüum na√Īcai pu√Īcai kaŠĻ≠amai kuŠĻ≠im
ai UŠł∑paŠĻ≠a veŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠itaŠĻČińĀŠł∑ ńÄyam pńĀŠĻ≠ikńĀval cilvari peruvari kńĀŠĻütikaiarici kńĀŠĻütikaippaccai maŠĻüŠĻüumuŠł∑Šł∑a pala kńĀcńĀyamkaŠł∑um taŠĻüiiŠĻüai kaŠĻ≠aiiŠĻüai taŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠ńĀrppńĀŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠a√Īce
kkukkaŠĻ≠amaiyum ńÄcivakakńĀcu EppeŠĻüpaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠a AŠĻČaittńĀyaŠĻÖkaŠł∑um ńÄyańĀya looks like ńĀyi. ń™ŠĻČńĀr ciŠĻüai IrńĀmaŠĻČ keraŠł∑aŠĻČ pakkal poŠĻČŠĻČaŠĻüakkoŠĻáŠĻ≠u maŠĻáŠĻáaŠĻüappoykai AruŠł∑ńĀŠł∑apperumńĀŠł∑ukkut
tiruviŠĻ≠aiyńĀŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠amńĀka cantarńĀRead cantirńĀ. tittavarai celvatńĀka viŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠eŠĻČ ceŠĻÖkeŠĻái virńĀcaŠĻČi AmmaiyappaŠĻČ taŠĻČiŠĻČiŠĻČŠĻüu veŠĻČŠĻüńĀŠĻČ taŠĻČ vaci kńĀŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠uvńĀŠĻČ AŠłĽakiya coŠłĽaŠĻČńĀŠĻČa Etirili

Hail! Prosperity! [In the month of] Tai of the twenty-second year of the illustrious Tribhuvanachakravartin, the illustrious RńĀjarńĀja-deva, which was current during the Ňöaka year one thousand one hundred and sixty,‚ÄĒI, ŇöeŠĻÖgeŠĻái-VńęrńĀŇõani-AmmaiyappaŠĻČ, who has gained victory standing by himself, who shows his sword, AŠĻõagiya-ŇöoŠĻõaŠĻČ, alias Edirili ‚ÄĘ ‚ÄĘ ‚ÄĘ ‚ÄĘ ‚ÄĘ , after having received gold from RńĀma the KeraŠł∑a, a slave (i.e., worshipper) of ńÄya-ńęŠĻČńĀr,This is probably a name of VishŠĻáu. It might mean: ‚Äúhe who is without a mother,‚ÄĚ and correspond to the Sanskrit Aja, ‚Äúthe unborn.‚ÄĚ Or could it be a corruption of aiyaŠĻČńĀr, a forest deity of the Tamil districts? ‚ÄĒgave to the VishŠĻáu temple of Chitra-meŠĻõi-MalaimaŠĻáŠłćala, alias (the temple of) AruŠł∑ńĀŠł∑a-PerumńĀŠł∑, (at) Poygai, alias RńĀjendra-ŇöoŠĻõanallŇęr, (the village of) KumńĀra-maŠĻÖgalam as exclusive property,See page 69, note 2. to last as long as the moon and the sun;‚ÄĒthe boundary on the western side is to the east of the tiruvńĀŠĻõiAccording to the Dictionnaive Tamoul-Fran√ßais, tiruvńĀŠłĽi means ‚Äúa royal seal.‚ÄĚ stone put up at the extremity of the boundary of KoŠĻüŠĻüa-maŠĻÖgalam; the boundary on the southern side is to the north of the channel of AimbŇęŠĻáŠłći, which lies to the north of Poygai, alias RńĀjendra-ŇöoŠĻõa-nallŇęr; the boundary on the eastern side is to the west of the tiruvńĀŠĻõi stone put up at the extremity of the boundary of AimbŇęŠĻáŠłći; the boundary on the northern side is to the south of the (river) PńĀlńĀŠĻüu;‚ÄĒthe trees overground and the wells underground, the wet land and the dry land, included within these boundaries in the four directions; including taxes and rights; (the revenue for) one VeŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠i,See page 82, note 3. tolls (ńĀyam), the small taxes (and) the large taxes for the village-police, the rice in KńĀrttika, the unripe (fruit) in KńĀrttika, and all other revenue in money; the tax on looms, the tax on shops, the tax on goldsmiths, the tax on oil-mills, the tax on ńÄjńęvakas,The ńÄjńęvakas are the Jainas In stead of ńĀcivakakńĀcu we read ńĀcuvakakaŠĻ≠amai in three other Poygai inscriptions and ńĀcuvikaŠĻČ perkkaŠĻ≠amai in the PaŠłćaveŠłću inscription No. 78. and all (other) revenue.
Digital edition of SII 1.59 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
